1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

48	    where `Pi.lift_thing := Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ Pi.π Z₁ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x)).op`.
49	
50	    **Mathematical structure of the proof** (~20–40 LOC):
51	    1. The categorical scalar `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing` is k-linear (the
52	       `(-1)^↑i` lives in k via `Linear k (ModuleCat k)`); pinning the
53	       scalar's k-type unblocks `Linear.smul_comp` / `ModuleCat.hom_smul`.
54	    2. Once `(-1)^↑i •` is extracted past the `.hom` and past `eqToHom.hom`
55	       and past `e₂` (all k-linear operations), the goal reduces to:
56	       `e₂ (eqToHom.hom (Pi.lift_thing.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))) =
57	        r' • e₂ (eqToHom.hom (Pi.lift_thing.hom (e₁.symm y')))`
58	       (after canceling `(-1)^↑i •` symmetrically on both sides via
59	       `smul_comm` if needed).
60	    3. The reduced equation is per-coordinate R-linearity of the composite
61	       `e₂ ∘ eqToHom.hom ∘ Pi.lift_thing.hom ∘ e₁.symm`: each output
62	       coordinate `j' : Fin (n+1) → ↥s₀` is `presheaf.map (...).hom`-applied
63	       to `(r' • y') (j' ∘ δ i)`, which `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`
64	       (L425, fully proved iter-087) factorizes as a single restriction map
65	       `R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ j')` followed by
66	       R-action via `RingHom.toModule` — this commutes with `r' •`.
67	
68	    **Recommended tactic chain** (iter-100 prover should verify each step at
69	    the LSP):
70	    ```lean
71	    intro i _ r' y'
72	    -- Step (a): pin scalar to k via `set` BEFORE distributing through .hom
73	    set h_sgn : k := (-1)^(↑i : ℕ) with h_sgn_def
74	    -- Step (b): extract scalar via Linear.smul_comp on the morphism level
75	    rw [show ((-1 : ?) ^ ↑i • Pi.lift_thing) = h_sgn • Pi.lift_thing from rfl]
76	    rw [Linear.smul_comp]   -- (h_sgn • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom = h_sgn • (Pi.lift_thing ≫ eqToHom)
77	    -- Step (c): distribute through .hom + LinearMap.smul_apply
78	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply]
79	    -- Step (d): push h_sgn past e₂ via map_smul (e₂ is k-linear)
80	    rw [map_smul e₂.toLinearMap h_sgn _]
81	    rw [map_smul e₂.toLinearMap h_sgn _]
82	    -- Step (e): cancel h_sgn symmetrically via smul_left_comm
83	    rw [smul_left_comm h_sgn r' _]
84	    congr 1
85	    -- Step (f): residual R-linearity of `e₂ ∘ eqToHom.hom ∘ Pi.lift_thing.hom ∘ e₁.symm`
86	    -- Goal: e₂ (eqToHom.hom (Pi.lift_thing.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))) =
87	    --         r' • e₂ (eqToHom.hom (Pi.lift_thing.hom (e₁.symm y')))
88	    -- Approach: `funext j'`-equivalent extraction via Pi.π evaluation, then
89	    -- `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` per-coordinate.
90	    ext j'
91	    simp only [Pi.smul_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply,
92	               ModuleCat.eqToHom_hom, Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
93	    -- Reduces to: presheaf.map _.op (presheaf.map _.op r' * y' (j' ∘ δ i)) =
94	    --             r' * presheaf.map _.op (presheaf.map _.op (y' (j' ∘ δ i)))
95	    -- Use map_mul + presheafMap_restrict_collapse to commute r'-action.
96	    rw [presheafMap_restrict_collapse, map_mul]
97	    rfl
98	    ```
99	
100	    **Fallback ladder** if the recommended chain breaks at any step:
101	
102	    - **(F1) `set` misses scalar pinning**: try `change (((-1 : k)^(↑i : ℕ)) • _) ...` directly with explicit type ascription, OR use a `have h_eq : ((-1)^(↑i : ℕ) : k) = h_sgn := rfl` followed by `rw [h_eq]` at the offending position.
103	    - **(F2) `Linear.smul_comp` doesn't fire**: try `Preadditive.zsmul_comp` with `Int.cast` bridge `((-1 : ℤ)^(↑i : ℕ)).toLinearMap`; OR show the smul-distribution manually via `LinearMap.ext` + per-element computation.
104	    - **(F3) `map_smul e₂.toLinearMap h_sgn _` HOU-fails**: explicitly apply `e₂.map_smul h_sgn _` on each side, or use `(e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂) →ₗ[k] (∀ j, Z₂ j)).map_smul`.
105	    - **(F4) `ext j'` fails on the LinearEquiv image**: replace with `LinearEquiv.injective e₂.symm` (or `LinearEquiv.injective e₂`) + `funext j'`-style coordinate extraction at the Pi-level.
106	    - **(F5) per-coordinate `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` doesn't close**: open the per-coordinate goal with `change` showing the explicit `presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op` chain, then apply `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` with the precise `V W U` triple from the context.

292	- No new `axiom` declarations.
293	- No constant/vacuous shortcuts for protected definitions.
294	- No `≃` / `≃+` where `≃ₗ[k]` is required.
295	- Use `theorem` (not `instance`) for consumers taking explicit non-synthesisable arguments.
296	- Avoid `Scheme.` short-name prefix inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`.
297	- Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _` *unconditionally* — forbidden.
298	- Plan-agent must not pre-verify candidate proof bodies via `lean_run_code` / `lean_multi_attempt`; no chains of thin helper extraction across iterations.
299	- Future code opening `MonObj` must avoid `ι` as a binder name (scoped Mathlib notation for `GrpObj.inv`).
300	- **iter-086**: No helper with a universally-false signature may be introduced even with a `sorry` body. Example violation (iter-085): `lemma foo (S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claims every short complex is exact. When the genuine `iff`-form is not closeable, leave the inline `sorry` rather than displacing it via a `sorry`-bodied helper.
301	- **iter-086**: No 80+-line in-code comment blocks documenting dead-end tactics; if the lane stalls, write a SHORT task report and escalate to refactor rather than expanding the inline scaffolding.
302	- **iter-087**: When extracting an inline `have` to a top-level decl via refactor, the extracted signature MUST be concrete to the call site's specialized context. iter-087 first-pass `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` had a universally-false signature; specialize pass caught it.
303	- **iter-089**: When committing a multi-step tactic chain (here S6 = a,b,c,…h) without LSP feedback, do NOT wrap the whole chain in `first | <chain> | sorry` — that loses the "deepest committed step" signal needed for next-iter re-scoping. Commit each step linearly and put the trailing `sorry` after the deepest committed step.
304	- **iter-090**: For multi-step tactic chains under LSP-unavailable conditions, prefer universal `simp only [map_sum]` over direct `rw [foo.map_sum, …]` for AddMonoidHomClass distribution — `simp only` tolerates HOU pattern variants and iterates through nested wrappers (`eqToHom`, `Function.comp`, etc.). Also: when both sides of an equation are sums but the RHS has `r • ∑` shape, prefer forward-direction `simp only [Finset.smul_sum]` BEFORE `Finset.sum_congr` over the original end-of-chain `rw [← Finset.smul_sum]` — mathematically equivalent, structurally cleaner. The per-step fallback ladder is now refined to per-step granularity (each step in the chain is its own commit point).
305	- **iter-092**: `sorry_analyzer.py` counts syntactic `sorry` tokens but does NOT verify compilation. When mathlib was missing in the sandbox (iter-086 → iter-091), only `sorry_analyzer.py` ran for verification, and false positives accumulated. **Plan-agent verification step: ALWAYS run `lake build <target>` or `lean_diagnostic_messages` as part of the verification pass; do NOT trust `sorry_analyzer.py` alone for compilation status.** When the sandbox is missing mathlib, `lake exe cache get` from project root + `$HOME/.elan/bin` on PATH fetches it; this works in iter-092+. The prover under LSP-unavailable conditions should NOT commit tactic chains beyond a single step — the iter-091 bare-commit-without-LSP pattern stacks unverified work and produces false-positive sorry counts.
306	- **iter-092**: `letI x : T := v` in CONCLUSION position of a `theorem ... :  ... := by ...` is silently zeta-reduced AWAY during elaboration if `x` doesn't appear explicitly in the rest of the conclusion. The instance synthesis hint provided by the letI is BAKED INTO downstream `•`/`*`/`⬝`-style instance uses, but the let-binder `x` does NOT survive. `intro` cannot name what isn't there. **Idiom for the pattern "introduce instances in the statement so the conclusion typechecks, then access them by name in the body":** move the `letI` block from conclusion-position to body-position (right after `intro` of the let-binders that the letI bodies depend on). Both forms compute the same baked-in instance; the body-position form keeps the name in scope for downstream tactics.
307	- **iter-093**: When `simp only [lem]` / `rw [lem]` fails with "made no progress" or "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a goal that visibly contains the LHS of `lem`, the cause is usually HOU pattern matching failure (the summand or closure body references the bound index in two or more positions). Standard escalation ladder: (1) try `simp_rw [lem]` first — Lean's `simp_rw` is more tolerant of HOU patterns than `simp only`; (2) if `simp_rw` also fails, build the equation manually via an explicit `AddMonoidHom` / `RingHom` / `LinearMap` and apply `map_sum` / `map_mul` / `map_smul` — these `map_*` lemmas operate on the structure homomorphism rather than via discrimination-tree HOU; (3) as a last resort, use `Finset.cons_induction` with per-step `Finset.sum_cons` + `map_add` (or the relevant additivity lemma) + IH.
308	- **iter-094**: `LinearMap.comp_apply` (`rfl`-lemma: `(f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x)`) does NOT fire if `g`'s codomain and `f`'s source are def-equal but NOT syntactically equal. Symptoms: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress"; `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` not found"; `rw [show (f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x) from rfl]` reports same. Cause: the discrimination tree's unification fails to identify two def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct types (e.g. `∏ᶜ Z₂` vs `ModuleCat.of k (Pi Z₂)`). **Escalation ladder**: (1) try `LinearMap.ext` to lift the equality OUT of the `∘ₗ`-application — at the `intro x` binder, Lean elaborates the codomain explicitly, bypassing the discrimination tree; (2) eliminate the source of the mismatch (e.g., drop the simp lemma that introduced the def-equal unfolding from the simp set; replace with explicit `Eq.mpr` cast); (3) restructure to push the offending wrapping outside the sum at a higher level of abstraction (categorical, not LinearMap).
309	- **iter-095**: When iter-094's `LinearMap.comp_apply` failure is structural (def-equal-not-syntactic-equal codomain/source), the iter-094 escape via `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (`(f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom` IN REVERSE) FUSES the `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` LinearMap composition into the categorical `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — at the categorical level, `≫` accepts def-equal types where the LinearMap discrimination tree did not. **Pattern**: when a LinearMap `f ∘ₗ g` rewrite is HOU-blocked, try fusing into a categorical `g' ≫ f'` via `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` (or analogous `← Functor.map_comp`-style lemmas for other categories).
310	- **iter-095**: HOU pattern matching on a `Preadditive.sum_comp` / `key₂`-style lemma where the summand body has nested references to the outer summation index in non-Miller positions (e.g., `(-1)^↑i`, `SimplexCategory.δ i` applied multiple times) is fundamentally blocked. The lemma may be provable (HOU-friendly proof with free variables) but APPLYING it via `rw`/`simp` fails. **Escalation ladder**: (1) `set F : Fin (n+1) → (X ⟶ Y) := fun i => <summand body> with hF`; the `set` substitutes the body with `F i` in the goal, so subsequent `rw`s have no HOU. Type ascription is CRITICAL — for `(-1)^↑i • <body>`, use `(((-1 : ℤ))^(i : ℕ) : ℤ) • <body>` with explicit casts. May need a preceding `change`/`show` to fold let-bound symbols (e.g. `Z₁`) back from their unfolded form. (2) Push the offending wrapping outside via a second `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to absorb outer `(Pi.π ...)`-style factors, then apply the distribution with an `i`-independent `?g`. (3) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` to peel summands manually — each cons-step has the fresh index as a HYPOTHESIS (not a binder), so HOU vanishes. **iter-095 EXTENSION** (iter-096 outcome): ALL THREE tactic-level routes proved structurally blocked in practice — (G) `set F` fails because `change`/`show`-fold of `Z₁` would need to match an eta-mismatched literal form; (H) `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` / `← CategoryTheory.comp_apply` fail on def-equal-not-syntactic Pi-product / unfolded carrier types; (I) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` fails typeclass synthesis (`Fintype ?m`). When all three fail, the route forward is to **extract an abstract structural lemma whose summand family is a single lemma-level binder `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)`**: applying that lemma uses first-order Miller unification (`?F := fun i ↦ <summand body>`) — fundamentally distinct from `rw [key₂]`'s discrimination-tree HOU, which fails on the same summand.
311	- **iter-096**: When a refactor agent tries to bake a complex `let φ_i := ...` expression into a lemma's SIGNATURE, and the body of the `let` contains type-level coercions like `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` that need to unify with `Fin n → ↥s₀` (the body's actual type post-`dif_pos hRel`), Lean's elaborator will deterministically `whnf` timeout (no tactic state to apply `dif_pos`). **Workaround pattern**: state the helper at the simpler index type and let the call site rewrite to bridge; OR keep the summand body abstract (as a lemma binder rather than a `let`-binding); OR inline the proof entirely instead of factoring through the helper. The iter-096 refactor confirmed: abstract structural lemmas (with the family `F` as a binder) elaborate cleanly; per-summand helpers with literal Čech bodies baked in their signatures do not.
312	- **iter-096**: When iter-095's tactic-level routes (G/H/I) ALL fail with structural reasons documented at the LSP level, the iter-096 escalation pattern is: extract an abstract structural lemma whose conclusion's UNIFICATION against the goal is Miller-friendly. The lemma's body is a separate proof obligation (sorry-filled by next-iter prover), and so is the per-summand discharge at the call site. This trades 1 syntactic sorry for 2 syntactic sorries — but EACH is HOU-free because the family is named, so the prover can fill both routinely. The iter-094 `key₂` body-local helper had the same intent but body-locally; promoting it to top-level moves the application's HOU into first-order Miller unification (the Lean kernel handles it).

737	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
738	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
739	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
740	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
741	schemes in our setting.
742	
743	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
744	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
745	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
746	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
747	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
748	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
749	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
750	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
751	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
752	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
753	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
754	
755	The structural skeleton is:
756	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
757	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
758	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
759	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
760	
761	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
762	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
763	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
764	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
765	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
766	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
767	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
768	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
769	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
770	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
771	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
772	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
773	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
774	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
775	
776	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
777	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/

301	    [Module R Y] : (X ≃ₗ[R] Y) ≅ (ModuleCat.of R X ≅ ModuleCat.of R Y) where
302	  hom := TypeCat.ofHom (fun e ↦ e.toModuleIso)
303	  inv := TypeCat.ofHom (fun i ↦ i.toLinearEquiv)

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

variable {k : Type} [Field k]

example {M N : ModuleCat.{0} k} (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N) (x : M) :
    (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]
  rfl

example {ι : Type} {Z : ι → ModuleCat.{0} k} {M : ModuleCat.{0} k} (n : ℤ)
    (f : M ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z) (x : M) :
    (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]
  rfl

  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]
  rfl

-- Now with a Pi.lift on the smul:
example {ι : Type} {Z : ι → ModuleCat.{0} k} [HasProduct Z]
    {M : ModuleCat.{0} k} (n : ℤ)
    (g : ∀ i, M ⟶ Z i) (x : M) :
    ((n • (Pi.lift g : M ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z))).hom x = n • (Pi.lift g).hom x := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

variable {k : Type} [Field k]

-- Test: can we rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul] when the smul is (-1)^↑i for i : Fin ?
example {n : ℕ} {ι : Type} {Z₁ Z₂ : ι → ModuleCat.{0} k} [HasProduct Z₁] [HasProduct Z₂]
    (i : Fin (n + 2)) (f : ∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z₂) (x : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)) :
    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (↑i : ℕ) • f).hom x = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (↑i : ℕ) • f.hom x := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

variable {k : Type} [Field k]

example {n : ℕ} {ι : Type} {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : ι → ModuleCat.{0} k} [HasProduct Z₁] [HasProduct Z₂] [HasProduct Z₃]
    (i : Fin (n + 2)) (f : ∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z₂)
    (g : ∏ᶜ Z₂ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z₃)
    (x : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)) (out : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)) :
    g.hom (((-1 : ℤ) ^ (↑i : ℕ) • f).hom x) = out := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]
  sorry

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

variable {k : Type} [Field k]

-- The actual goal has Pi.lift_thing IN-PLACE (not as a binder). Mimic that.
example {n : ℕ} {ι : Type} (Z₁ : (Fin (n+1) → ι) → ModuleCat.{0} k)
    (Z_int : (Fin (n+2) → ι) → ModuleCat.{0} k)
    [HasProduct Z₁] [HasProduct Z_int]
    (i : Fin (n + 2)) (δ : Fin (n+1) → Fin (n+2))
    (φ : ∀ (i_1 : Fin (n+1) → ι), ∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ Z_int (i_1 ∘ δ))
    (x : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)) (out : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_int)) :
    (((-1 : ℤ) ^ (↑i : ℕ) • Pi.lift (fun i_1 => φ i_1)).hom x : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_int)) = out := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]
  sorry

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## cechCofaceMap_pi_smul L728 `?hG` discharge (iter-100)

**Outcome:** PARTIAL — `funext j'` structural step landed inside the discharge.
Sorry count unchanged (6, hard cap met). File compiles. No new axioms.

### Attempt 1 — recommended chain S1–S6 (per PROGRESS directive)
- **S1 (`set h_sgn : k := (-1) ^ (↑i : ℕ)`)**: FAILED. The goal still shows
  `(-1) ^ ↑i •` post-`set`. **Diagnostic insight**: `set h_sgn : ℤ` DID
  substitute; `set h_sgn : k` did NOT. So the scalar elaborates in ℤ
  (Preadditive ZSMul on `(∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z_inner)`), not k. The recommendation
  to `set : k` was inaccurate; iter-100 plan should have used ℤ.
- **S2 (`rw [Linear.smul_comp]`)**: FAILED — pattern `(?r • ?f) ≫ ?g` not
  found. Same issue as iter-099.

### Attempt 2 — ℤ-scalar variant
- `set h_sgn : ℤ := (-1) ^ (↑i : ℕ)` substitutes successfully (goal shows
  `h_sgn • Pi.lift_thing`). But then **`rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]` FAILS** to
  find the pattern `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (?n • ?f)`. Same for `simp only`,
  `simp_rw`, `dsimp only`, and explicit `rw [h_test]` with locally bound
  `h_test : ∀ M N (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N), (n • f).hom = n • f.hom := rfl`.

### Attempt 3 — diagnostic via `lean_run_code`
Verified that `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` DOES fire in a closely-matched standalone
test:
```lean
example {ι : Type} {Z : ι → ModuleCat.{0} k} [HasProduct Z] {M : ModuleCat k}
    (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z) (x : M) :
    (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]   -- WORKS
```
And with `(-1)^↑i` scalar and `i : Fin (n + 2)`: also works. So the lemma
is genuinely rfl-applicable on similar shapes. The actual goal differs in
that the Pi.lift's codomain is an **anonymous closure**
`∏ᶜ (fun i_1 ↦ Pi.π Z₁_unfolded (i_1 ∘ δ i) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map ...)`.
The discrimination tree's pattern unification appears to fail through this
closure even though the lemma's instance synth would succeed.

### Attempt 4 — categorical-level extraction
- `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` (BEFORE the L727 simp): FAILED with pattern
  `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g` not found.
- `rw [Preadditive.nsmul_comp]`, `rw [CategoryTheory.Preadditive.zsmul_comp]`:
  same failure.
- `rw [Linear.smul_comp]`: same failure.

### Attempt 5 — `set f' := Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` to abstract the morphism
FAILED at the `set` step — the Pi.lift's body has elaboration-ambiguous
holes (the inner `Pi.lift fun x ↦ Pi.π (...)` can't be expressed without
explicit annotation, and the annotation collides with type-level coercions).

### Attempt 6 — pivot to per-coordinate via `funext j'`
WORKED. After `funext j'`, the goal becomes per-coordinate at `Z₂ j'`:
```
e₂ (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))) j' =
  (r' • e₂ (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e₁.symm y')))) j'
```

**Committed chain at L726–L768** (L728 → L768 due to documentation comments):
```lean
intro i _ r' y'
simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
-- [extensive iter-100 diagnostic comments]
funext j'
sorry
```

The `funext j'` step is a real structural advance — the per-coordinate
equation is at the `↑(Z₂ j')` level where R-action is concretely via
`RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map _).hom`, and `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`
applies directly without needing to push `(-1)^↑i •` through the chain.

### Result: IN PROGRESS (1 structural step landed, residual is per-coord R-linearity)
- Sorry budget: 6 (hard cap), unchanged from iter-099.
- File compiles (`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.BasicOpenCech` succeeds).
- No new axioms.
- Sorry locations (verified via `grep -nE '^\\s*sorry\\s*$'`):
  - L768 (was L728, iter-100 shift +40 from comment lines)
  - L860 (was L820)
  - L1184 (was L1144)
  - L1212 (was L1172)
  - L1402 (was L1362)
  - L1431 (was L1391)

### Concrete next steps for iter-101+

The per-coordinate equation post-`funext j'` decomposes as follows (iter-101
prover should execute step-by-step at LSP):

1. **Push j' through `r' • _` on RHS**:
   `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` → `r' • e₂ (...) j'`.
2. **Push j' through `e₂` on both sides** via
   `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j'`:
   `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (eqToHom_hom (...)) = r' • (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (eqToHom_hom (...))`.
3. **Fuse `Pi.π Z₂ j' ∘ eqToHom_hom` into a single LinearMap** via
   `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` (categorical fuse): converts to
   `((smul_thing ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j')).hom (e₁.symm (r' • y'))`.
4. **eqToHom-naturality on `eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j'`**: this equals
   `Pi.π Z₂_unfolded j'` (where Z₂_unfolded is the anonymous closure form).
   At this point the `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing ≫ Pi.π Z₂_unfolded j'` should
   simplify via `Pi.lift_π_apply` to the j'-th summand of Pi.lift_thing
   (a single morphism, not a Pi.lift any more).
5. **j'-th summand R-linearity** is per-coordinate
   `(presheaf.map _).hom ∘ (Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ δ i)).hom`, which
   `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (L425, fully proved iter-087) handles
   directly.
6. **The `(-1)^↑i •` scalar persists symmetrically through all of these
   steps** (it commutes with k-linear projections and with R-action), so
   `congr 1` (or `mul_smul`/`smul_comm`) factors it out at the end.

### Mathlib lemmas confirmed available for iter-101
- `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` (rfl, but doesn't pattern-match in this context)
- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` (used iter-099)
- `Pi.smul_apply` (used iter-099)
- `Pi.lift_π_apply` (used iter-090)
- `← ModuleCat.hom_comp`, `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` (composition fuse)
- `Preadditive.zsmul_comp` (exists, but pattern fails here)
- `Linear.smul_comp` (exists, but pattern fails here)
- `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (project-local L425, fully proved iter-087)
- `LinearEquiv.injective`, `funext`

### Dead-ends to avoid (carried forward to iter-101+)
- `rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]` on the goal in its current form (post-L727 simp,
  pre-funext). The lemma is rfl but the pattern matcher can't find the
  occurrence through the Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain.
- `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul, LinearMap.smul_apply]`: same.
- `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]`, `rw [Linear.smul_comp]`: pattern unification
  fails on `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g`.
- `change e₂ ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom _)) ((((-1 : ℤ) ^ ↑i) •
  ModuleCat.Hom.hom (Pi.lift _)) ...)) = _`: the inner `_` placeholders
  can't be filled because the codomain types don't unify across the cast.
- `apply LinearEquiv.injective e₂.symm; funext j'`: doesn't work because
  the types of LHS and RHS of `LinearEquiv.injective`'s conclusion don't
  match through `e₂.symm` — the LHS becomes the unfolded `↑(∏ᶜ Z₂_unfolded)`
  but the RHS remains `e₂.symm (r' • e₂ ...)` which doesn't reduce.

### Suggested iter-101 plan-agent action
Either:
1. **Add `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`-equivalent at the per-coordinate level** —
   the per-coordinate scalar push-out via `LinearMap.smul_apply` should fire
   when the per-coordinate equation is fully exposed (no Pi.lift wrapping).
2. **Refactor `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` to use `Pi.lift_thing` as a let-binder
   at the body level**, so the family `G : ι' → (∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z_int)` in the
   iter-098 split-slot lemma is matched against the let-binder rather than
   the literal anonymous-closure. This sidesteps the discrimination-tree
   issue at the call site.
3. **Or insert a body-local `have h_scalar_extract` that explicitly proves
   `(h_sgn • Pi.lift_thing).hom = h_sgn • Pi.lift_thing.hom`** via direct
   case-by-case computation, then use `rw [h_scalar_extract]` instead of
   `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`.

Iter-101 plan-agent should reassess based on this report.

## iter-098 · parallel prover

The iter-100 plan recommended `set h_sgn : k := (-1)^(↑i : ℕ)` as Step S1.
At LSP, that `set` does NOT substitute (the scalar is in ℤ, not k); only

the directive, when the choice of scalar type is ambiguous. Cost: one
LSP roundtrip; benefit: prover doesn't waste a route on a typed-incorrectly
`set`. Also: when a recommended chain depends on a Mathlib lemma being
rfl-applicable (here `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`), the plan agent could check
whether the lemma's discrimination-tree pattern is `?n • ?f` (simple) vs
something deeper-nested. Anonymous-closure codomains (`Pi.lift fun ↦ ...`)
break the unifier even on rfl lemmas — this is a known Lean 4 brittleness
that no recommendation in the directive can route around.

Summary:

**Outcome**: PARTIAL. Sorry count unchanged at 6 (hard cap met), file compiles, no new axioms.

**Structural advance**: Committed `funext j'` after `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]` at L727. This pushes the discharge from product-level to per-coordinate level, where `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` applies directly to the R-linearity residual.

**Why iter-100's recommended chain stalled**: The recommended `set h_sgn : k := (-1)^(↑i : ℕ)` failed at the LSP because the scalar elaborates in ℤ (Preadditive ZSMul), not k. Switching to `set h_sgn : ℤ` succeeded in substituting, but then `rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]` (a rfl lemma applicable in standalone tests via `lean_run_code`) failed to find its pattern in the actual goal — the Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain blocks discrimination-tree pattern matching. Six tactic variants (hom_zsmul, hom_smul, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.nsmul_comp, Linear.smul_comp, dsimp only) all hit the same pattern-match wall.

**For iter-101**: Per-coordinate chain at L768 sorry, with explicit 6-step plan in `.archon/task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md` using `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` + `Pi.lift_π_apply` + `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. Three alternate refactor approaches suggested.
